title: pb_skip_value does not handle unknown wire types safely
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.c:372-401
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/codec.c:271-300

pb_skip_value handles wire types 0,1,2,5 but returns -EINVAL for others:
  default:
      return -EINVAL;

Wire type 3 and 4 are deprecated start/end group markers from proto2. A malicious input using wire type 3 or 4 would cause parse failure even if otherwise valid.

More critically, wire type 6 and 7 are reserved/undefined. The current code correctly rejects them, but the comment should document WHY they're rejected (undefined behavior in protobuf spec).

PROTOCOL: Rejecting unknown wire types is correct per protobuf spec, but adding a comment would improve maintainability.
close_reason: Fixed: Added PB_WT_SGROUP/EGROUP constants and explicit handling for deprecated proto2 wire types 3 and 4 with SECURITY comments. Also documented why wire types 6/7 are rejected. Changes in both adapter.c and codec.c.
